commandDescription: Manage the bridge between two discord channels
createDescription: Create a bridge between two discord channels
confirmDescription: Confirm a bridge creation between two discord channels
pauseDescription: Pause a bridge between two discord channels
resumeDescription: Resume a bridge between two discord channels
infoDescription: Get info on this bridge setup
removeDescription: Remove a bridge between two discord channels
externalChannelOption: Channel ID on the other guild
noBridgeError: Error: No bridges found for this channel.
noPermissionCreate: Error: You do not have the ManageChannel permission needed to create a bridge!
notTripSit:
Error: This command can only be used in the TripSit Discord server.
You likely want to use the /bridge confirm command instead!
notTextChannel: Error: Internal channel is not a text channel.
missingPermission:
Error: Missing {{permission}} permission in {{guild}}'s {{channel}}!
Manage Webhooks - Create the channel webhook
invalidChannelId: Error: External channel ID is not a number!
channelNotFound: Error: This channel ID does not exist, did you enter the ID correctly?
externalNotTextChannel: Error: External channel is not a text channel.
externalMissingPermission:
Error: Missing {{permission}} permission in {{guild}}'s {{channel}}!

Ask the collaborator to make sure the bot has the right permissions:
View Channel - To see the channel
Send Messages - To send a message to the other guild
Manage Webhooks - To create the channel webhook
createRequestDescription:
{{member}} has requested a bridge between {{internalGuild}}'s {{internalChannel}} and {{externalGuild}}'s {{externalChannel}}!
If you want to accept this bridge, please use the **/bridge confirm** command here.
If this is not expected you can ignore this as it may be a mistake.
If this command is being abused please talk to Moonbear on TripSit's Discord guild.
createSuccess:
Initializing bridge between {{internalGuild}}'s {{internalChannel}} and {{externalGuild}}'s {{externalChannel}}!
I've prompted the other guild to confirm the bridge.
noPermissionPause: Error: You do not have the ManageChannel permission needed to pause a bridge!
noActiveBridges: Error: No active bridges found for this channel. Did you mean to use the /bridge resume command?
pausedMultiple: Paused {{count}} bridge(s) connected to this room.
pausedSingle: Paused bridge connected to this room.
noPermissionResume: Error: You do not have the ManageChannel permission needed to resume a bridge!
noPausedBridges: Error: No paused bridges found for this channel. Did you mean to use the /bridge pause command?
resumedMultiple: Resumed {{count}} bridge(s) connected to this room.
resumedSingle: Resumed bridge connected to this room.
noPermissionRemove: Error: You do not have the ManageChannel permission needed to remove a bridge!
confirmRemovalMultiple:
Error: Are you sure you want to remove {{count}} bridge(s) connected to this room?

If so, please use the confirmation option with your guild id.
confirmRemovalSingle:
Error: Are you sure you want to remove this bridge?

If so, please use the confirmation option using your guild ID as the confirmation code!
removedMultiple: Removed {{count}} bridge(s) connected to this room.
removedSingle: Removed bridge connected to this room.
noPermissionInfo: Error: You do not have the ManageChannel permission needed to info a bridge!
infoMultiple: This room is connected to {{count}} other rooms:
guildOnly: This command can only be used in a guild.
partnerOnly:
This command can only be used in a partner guild!
If you are a partner and this is an error, please contact Moonbear.
If you are not a partner, tell Moonbear you're interested:
This is a new system and we're still figuring out how it works.
confirmDescription: Confirm a bridge between two discord channels
confirmSuccess:
{{member}} has confirmed the bridge between {{externalGuild}}'s {{externalChannel}} and {{internalGuild}}'s {{internalChannel}}!
Either side can */bridge pause* and */bridge resume* to temporarily pause/resume the bridge, or */bridge delete* to remove the bridge.
This is the start of something beautiful, say hi!
